description: Complete inventory of all BMEDIS Copilot AI pipeline files

files.purpose: Next.js API route handler for POST /api/chat — entry point for all copilot requests
files.reason: Single gateway; any breakage here kills all AI responses. Builds UserChatProfile including role loading.

files.purpose: Regex-based intent classifier and capability router. Maps user message to ChatIntent + CapabilityId via pattern matching.
files.reason: Core of the routing problem. Regex-only classifier with broad troubleshooting patterns that steal summary/status queries. Default fallback is maintenance_tip, not a neutral catch-all.

files.purpose: Defines all 25 CapabilityDefinition records with intent mappings, required inputs, retrieval blocks, response modes, and safety constraints.
files.reason: Registry is well-structured but every capability uses ALL_SECTIONS for responseSections, removing any differentiation of output shape per capability.

files.purpose: Evaluates safety decision (answer/limited_answer/check_manual/escalate/refuse) based on intent, role, evidence, and content patterns.
files.reason: Safety logic is thorough but troubleshooting/calibration intents always return limited_answer even when evidence is available, which degrades summary quality.

files.purpose: Builds the system prompt and user prompt payload sent to Gemini. Contains CHATBOT_SYSTEM_PROMPT and outputContract.
files.reason: System prompt contains 'For troubleshooting, provide safe first-line checks only' as a global instruction, not scoped to the troubleshooting capability. This bleeds into all response types.

files.purpose: Builds ChatEvidence by querying Supabase for equipment, work orders, departments, maintenance history, PM, calibration, logistics, analytics, manuals, and document embeddings.
files.reason: Solid data loading but logistics/analytics are gated on specific intent values, meaning some capabilities get less context than they need.

files.purpose: Main orchestration function that chains classifier -> entity resolution -> task context -> safety -> deterministic builders -> provider call -> response normalization -> usefulness guard -> claim verification -> telemetry.
files.reason: Central coordination point. Well-structured but very long (~1000 lines). Contains page-aware capability override logic that is a secondary routing layer.

files.purpose: Thin wrapper that delegates to the active chat provider (Gemini).
files.reason: Simple delegation; risk is in the provider implementation, not here.

files.purpose: Gemini API provider implementation. Handles model call, JSON parsing, and fallback.
files.reason: Provider works but parser recovery path can lose structured data and fall back to generic text.

files.purpose: Provider factory that returns GeminiProvider based on env config.
Normalizes raw provider output into AssistantContent, ensures UI safety, builds AI-unavailable fallback.
files.reason: Good safety net but can mask useful data when normalization strips fields.

files.purpose: Parses and normalizes raw provider content (JSON or text) into validated AssistantContent. Multi-candidate JSON scorer.
files.reason: Robust parser with multi-candidate scoring but format_recovery path produces generic responses.

files.purpose: Builds deterministic structured fallback when provider fails or parser recovery is needed, using context blocks directly.
files.reason: Good fallback system but limited to a few capabilities (prioritize_tasks, safe_troubleshooting, summarize_work_order).

files.purpose: Builds deterministic (non-LLM) answers for each capability using retrieved tool/context data. 1200+ lines covering 15+ capability builders.
files.reason: Excellent system-data-grounded builders. The usefulness guard prefers these over generic LLM output. But not all capabilities have builders yet.

files.purpose: Post-processing guard that replaces generic/unhelpful LLM output with deterministic answers when evidence is available.
files.reason: Critical quality gate but relies on looksGeneric() heuristics which may miss some generic patterns.

files.purpose: Verifies LLM claims against retrieved evidence and removes unsupported claims.
files.reason: Good grounding verification but only operates on final output.

files.purpose: Builds role-specific prompt policy (behavior, tone) for each CopilotRoleCategory.
files.reason: Well-designed role differentiation. Uses proper Menilik role names.

files.purpose: Copilot-specific RBAC helpers: role category mapping, capability blocks, department scope, draft permissions.
files.reason: Clean RBAC implementation using correct role model (developer, admin, bme_head, technician, store_user, department_head, department_user, viewer).

files.purpose: Detects prompt injection attempts and unsafe biomedical requests via regex patterns. Provides structured refusal copy.
files.reason: Well-designed with clear categories, safe first-line alternatives, and honest refusal messages.

files.purpose: Builds TaskContextBundle by running tool plans, loading shared/risk/logistics/decision-support blocks, and assembling formal tool results.
files.reason: Complex context assembly with dual tool systems (legacy + formal). Intent-to-evidence mapping uses hardcoded ternary chain.

files.purpose: Plans which legacy tools to invoke based on classified request.
files.reason: Legacy tool planner; formal tool planner in task-context-service is the primary path.

files.purpose: Exports all tool functions (getCurrentUserContext, getMyTasks, getEquipmentSummary, etc.).
files.reason: Data loaders that feed the context pipeline. Quality of answers depends on what these return.

files.purpose: Executes formal copilot tools by name, returning structured CopilotToolResult.
files.reason: Central formal tool execution. RBAC-gated per tool.

files.purpose: Registry of formal copilot tools with metadata, RBAC requirements, and route links.
TypeScript types for formal copilot tool system.
Shared data loaders for task context (loadTaskBlocks, loadRiskAndAnalytics, etc.).
files.reason: Core data loading functions that populate the context blocks.

files.purpose: Tool: retrieves equipment summary with analytics, history, and status.
Tool: retrieves work order summary with asset context.
Tool: retrieves department readiness snapshot.
Tool: builds safe troubleshooting context from evidence.
Tool: returns current user context for copilot grounding.
Tool: retrieves inventory/logistics stock status.
Tool: retrieves procurement pipeline status.
Builds Tier1TroubleshootingBundle with safe checklist, hypothesis buckets, and evidence references.
files.reason: Clean, bounded troubleshooting builder. Equipment-type-aware checklist.

files.purpose: Loads and persists conversation memory (rolling summary, thread intent, active capability, last entities).
Resolves entities (equipment, work order, department, part) from message text, context refs, module context, and memory.
files.reason: Entity resolution feeds contextRefs to loaders. Weak resolution causes missing context.

files.purpose: Generates proactive signals, alert synthesis, and cross-module snapshot for enrichment.
Resolves data freshness, mode (live/snapshot/stale/sandbox), and age labels.
Logs copilot telemetry events to Supabase.
Tracks and limits copilot usage (token counting, hard limits).
Usage limit constants and threshold logic.
Default response metadata (title, intelligence_mode, follow_up_suggestions) per capability.
Additional response normalization utilities.
Client-side chat service for sending messages to /api/chat.
Deterministic workflow explanation answers (how to create requests, track work orders, etc.).
Handles short follow-up prompts ('why?', 'explain simply', 'where did you get that?') using previous turn context.
Builds route links for assistant responses.
Builds copilot action drafts (maintenance request, work order note, etc.) when user explicitly asks.
files.reason: Action draft builder. Correctly gated on explicit user request.

files.purpose: Evaluation dataset for capability classification testing.
Evaluation harness for running capability classification tests.
All TypeScript types for the chatbot system: intents, capabilities, decisions, evidence, profiles, schemas.
files.reason: Type system. 32 intents and 25 capabilities defined here.

files.purpose: Types for copilot action drafts.
UI button that opens the assistant panel with a seed prompt and context refs.
files.reason: Entry point button. Passes moduleLabel, seedPrompt, and contextRefs.

files.purpose: Main assistant slide-out panel with message list, input, quick prompts, and usage display.
files.reason: UI rendering. Contains QUICK_PROMPTS_BY_MODULE and QUICK_PROMPTS_BY_ROLE. Renders AssistantMessageCard.

files.purpose: React context provider for assistant state (open/close, messages, session, context refs).
Renders a single assistant message with structured sections (summary, findings, actions, troubleshooting steps, etc.).
files.reason: Key rendering component. Determines how structured data is displayed to user.

files.purpose: Displays context chips (equipment, work order, department) in the assistant panel.
Bridge component that passes page-level context (route, module, selected record) into the assistant provider.
files.reason: Critical for page-aware context. If this misses context, the copilot loses page awareness.

files.purpose: Renders copilot action draft cards with confirm/dismiss.
Confirmation dialog for copilot actions.
UI display helpers for assistant messages.
Offline copilot fallback behavior.
Dedicated chatbot page route.
Developer lab page with copilot diagnostics.
Client component for copilot diagnostics in developer lab.
Section component for copilot diagnostics display.
Main developer lab client component.
API route for Gemini smoke test (developer diagnostics).
Server actions for copilot action execution (drafts, mutations).
Server actions for copilot diagnostics.
Server actions for developer lab features.
Developer lab service layer.
Equipment document embedding search (pgvector/Gemini embeddings).
Demo role validation utilities for developer lab.
React hook for current user role.
